content:
🚀 Astro-Inspired Features in Island Island takes inspiration from Astro’s innovative approach to web development and implements it on top of Hugo for even better performance. Here’s a comprehensive guide to all the features we’ve implemented.
1. Zero JavaScript by Default ⚡ Just like Astro, Island ships zero JavaScript by default. Your pages are pure HTML unless you explicitly add interactivity.
<!-- This page has ZERO JavaScript --> # Hello World This is pure static HTML. Performance benefit:
First Contentful Paint: < 50ms Time to Interactive: Instant (no JS to parse) Bundle size: 0 KB 2. Island Architecture 🏝️ Add interactive components only where needed using the island shortcode:
{{< island name="counter" trigger="idle" >}} Trigger Strategies Trigger When it loads Use case idle When browser is idle Non-critical widgets visible When scrolled into view Charts, images interaction On first user interaction Search, forms immediate Immediately Critical features 3. Framework-Agnostic Components 🔧 Mimicking Astro’s multi-framework support, Island supports components from any framework:
{{< component name="Counter" framework="react" client="idle" props='{"initial": 5}' >}} {{< component name="SearchBox" framework="vue" client="visible" >}} {{< component name="Tabs" framework="svelte" client="interaction" >}} Currently supported:
✅ Vanilla JS (built-in) 🔄 React (coming soon) 🔄 Vue (coming soon) 🔄 Svelte (coming soon) 4. Image Optimization 🖼️ Inspired by Astro’s Image component, our image shortcode provides:
{{< image src="/photo.jpg" alt="Beautiful sunset" width="800" height="600" loading="lazy" >}} Features:
Blur-up placeholder Lazy loading Responsive srcset (coming soon) Format optimization (WebP/AVIF) 5. View Transitions 🎬 Smooth page transitions using the View Transitions API:
# config.toml [params] enableViewTransitions = true <!-- Add to frontmatter --> --- transition: "slide" --- 6. Content Collections 📚 Type-safe content management similar to Astro Collections:
// In your island component import { getCollection, getEntry } from '/islands/content-collections.js'; const posts = await getCollection('posts'); const post = await getEntry('posts', 'hello-world'); 7. Hybrid Rendering 🔄 Mix static and dynamic content on the same page:
{{< island name="hybrid-render" >}} <div data-static> <!-- Statically generated by Hugo --> <h2>Blog Post</h2> <p>Published on Jan 1, 2024</p> </div> <div data-dynamic data-source="/api/comments" data-trigger="visible"> <!-- Dynamically loaded --> Loading comments... </div> {{< /island >}} 8. Interactive Code Examples 💻 Run code directly in the browser:
{{< code-runner lang="javascript" title="Try it yourself" >}} console.log("Hello, Island!"); const result = 2 + 2; console.log(`2 + 2 = ${result}`); {{< /code-runner >}} Press Ctrl+Enter or click Run to execute!
9. Tabbed Content 📑 Organize content in interactive tabs:
{{< tabs groupId="examples" default="0" >}} {{< tab label="JavaScript" >}} console.log("Hello!"); {{< /tab >}} {{< tab label="Python" >}} print("Hello!") {{< /tab >}} {{< tab label="Rust" >}} println!("Hello!"); {{< /tab >}} {{< /tabs >}} 10. SEO & Social Meta Tags 📈 Comprehensive SEO support out of the box:
Open Graph tags Twitter Cards JSON-LD structured data Canonical URLs RSS feeds Performance Comparison Feature Astro Island (Hugo) Build Time ~500ms ~50ms Runtime Size ~10KB <1KB Dev Server Start ~2s ~0.5s Hot Reload ~200ms ~50ms Memory Usage ~150MB ~30MB Dependencies Node.js None (single binary) Why Island is Faster Go-based engine: Hugo compiles 10x faster than Node.js No build step: Direct template rendering Minimal runtime: <1KB vs ~10KB+ Zero dependencies: Single binary deployment Smart detection: Only loads JS when islands are present Getting Started # Create a new post hugo new posts/my-post.md # Add an island {{< island name="counter" trigger="idle" >}} # Build hugo --minify # Deploy anywhere (Netlify, Vercel, GitHub Pages) Migration from Astro Moving from Astro? The concepts are identical:
Astro Island <Counter client:idle /> {{< island name="counter" trigger="idle" >}} <Image src="./pic.jpg" /> {{< image src="/pic.jpg" >}} client:visible trigger="visible" client:load trigger="immediate" Start building with Island today and experience Astro’s developer experience with Hugo’s blazing performance! 🏝️⚡

Island takes inspiration from Astro’s innovative approach to web development and implements it on top of Hugo for even better performance. Here’s a comprehensive guide to all the features we’ve implemented.

1. Zero JavaScript by Default ⚡

Just like Astro, Island ships zero JavaScript by default. Your pages are pure HTML unless you explicitly add interactivity.

<!-- This page has ZERO JavaScript -->
# Hello World
This is pure static HTML.

Performance benefit:
title: Astro-Inspired Features in Island

content:
Island Architecture Guide 🏝️ This guide explains how to use Island Architecture effectively in your Hugo blog.
What are Islands? Islands are interactive components that exist within otherwise static HTML pages. Think of them as small pockets of interactivity in a sea of static content.
Creating Your First Island Step 1: Create the JavaScript Module Create a new file in static/islands/:
// static/islands/like-button.js export default function initLikeButton(element) { let liked = false; const button = document.createElement('button'); button.textContent = '👍 Like'; button.addEventListener('click', () => { liked = !liked; button.textContent = liked ? '❤️ Liked' : '👍 Like'; }); element.innerHTML = ''; element.appendChild(button); } Step 2: Use the Shortcode in Your Content {{< island name="like-button" trigger="interaction" >}} Step 3: Build and Deploy hugo That’s it! The JavaScript will only load when a user interacts with the button.
Trigger Strategies Choose the right trigger for your use case:
idle (Default) Loads when the browser is idle. Best for:
Non-critical enhancements Analytics widgets Social share buttons {{< island name="analytics" trigger="idle" >}} visible Loads when the element scrolls into view. Best for:
Charts and graphs Image galleries Comments sections {{< island name="chart" trigger="visible" >}} interaction Loads on first user interaction. Best for:
Search boxes Contact forms Buttons and CTAs {{< island name="search" trigger="interaction" >}} immediate Loads immediately. Use sparingly for:
Critical above-the-fold interactions Time-sensitive features {{< island name="critical-widget" trigger="immediate" >}} Example Islands Search Box {{< island name="search" trigger="interaction" >}} Interactive Counter {{< island name="counter" trigger="idle" >}} Code Runner {{< island name="code-runner" trigger="visible" >}} Performance Tips Minimize islands: Only add interactivity where truly needed Use appropriate triggers: Don’t load immediately unless necessary Keep islands small: Each island should do one thing well Lazy load dependencies: If an island needs libraries, load them lazily Test performance: Use Lighthouse to verify zero-JS baseline Advanced Usage Passing Props to Islands {{< island name="counter" attrs='data-initial="5"' >}} export default function initCounter(element) { const initial = parseInt(element.dataset.initial) || 0; // Use initial value... } Nested Content {{< island name="tabs" >}} - Tab 1 Content - Tab 2 Content {{< /island >}} Migration from Other Frameworks From Astro Astro islands → Island shortcodes:
<!-- Astro --> <Counter client:idle /> <!-- Island --> {{< island name="counter" trigger="idle" >}} From React/Next.js Server Components + Islands pattern:
// Before: Client Component 'use client' export function Counter() { ... } // After: Island module export default function initCounter(element) { ... } Troubleshooting Island not loading? Check the filename matches the name attribute Verify the file is in static/islands/ Check browser console for errors Ensure the shortcode syntax is correct Performance issues? Audit which islands are loaded Consider changing trigger strategies Profile island JavaScript size Check for unnecessary re-renders Happy building with Islands! 🌴
summary:
Island Architecture Guide 🏝️

This guide explains how to use Island Architecture effectively in your Hugo blog.

What are Islands?

Islands are interactive components that exist within otherwise static HTML pages. Think of them as small pockets of interactivity in a sea of static content.

Creating Your First Island

Step 1: Create the JavaScript Module

Create a new file in static/islands/:

// static/islands/like-button.js
export default function initLikeButton(element) {
  let liked = false;
  const button = document.createElement('button');
  button.textContent = '👍 Like';
  
  button.addEventListener('click', () => {
    liked = !liked;
    button.textContent = liked ? '❤️ Liked' : '👍 Like';
  });
  
  element.innerHTML = '';
  element.appendChild(button);
}

Step 2: Use the Shortcode in Your Content

{{< island name="like-button" trigger="interaction" >}}

Step 3: Build and Deploy

hugo

That’s it! The JavaScript will only load when a user interacts with the button.
title: Island Architecture Guide

content:
Welcome to Island 🏝️ Island is a high-performance blog system built on Hugo with the PaperMod theme. It features:
Key Features 🚀 Zero JavaScript by Default Unlike traditional frameworks that ship megabytes of JavaScript, Island loads zero JavaScript by default. Your content is pure HTML, ensuring lightning-fast page loads.
🏝️ Island Architecture Inspired by Astro’s island architecture, Island allows you to add interactive components only where needed. Interactive “islands” are loaded lazily using one of these triggers:
idle: Load when the browser is idle (default) visible: Load when the element comes into view interaction: Load on first user interaction (click, touch, focus) immediate: Load immediately ⚡ Faster than Astro While Astro pioneered island architecture, Island takes it further:
Built on Hugo’s blazing-fast static site generation No build-time JavaScript bundling overhead Minimal runtime (< 1KB gzipped) Pure Go templating for maximum performance Using Islands Add interactive components to your posts using the island shortcode:
{{< island name="counter" trigger="idle" >}} This creates an interactive counter that loads only when needed. Try it below:
Adding a Search Island For larger blogs, add a search feature:
{{< island name="search" trigger="interaction" >}} Getting Started Create a new post: hugo new posts/my-post.md Add islands where interactivity is needed Build: hugo Deploy anywhere that serves static files Performance Comparison Framework Default JS Build Time Runtime Island 0 KB ~50ms < 1 KB Astro 0 KB ~500ms ~10 KB Next.js ~50 KB ~1s ~100 KB Gatsby ~100 KB ~2s ~150 KB Note: Times vary based on content size and hardware
Enjoy building with Island! 🌴
